top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize the time to detect a glycol jacket blockage or partial restriction before fermenter temperature deviates >0.5°C from setpoint
Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading that masks a partially blocked glycol jacket
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible
Minimize batch-to-batch variation in fermentation temperature profile caused by uneven glycol distribution across cooling zones (target: <±0.5°C)
Minimize the number of undetected glycol pump degradation events that reduce per-zone cooling capacity by >15%

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of undetected calibration drift exceeding ±0.5% of reading over a 12-month service period
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW drifts 2–5% over life (dominant 50–60%); mag stable but minority; manual = zero measurement

needs.statement: Minimize variation in flow measurement accuracy caused by glycol concentration fluctuations between 25% and 40% propylene glycol
needs.satisfactionRationale: No incumbent compensates for glycol concentration changes

needs.statement: Increase the ability to measure flow rate across the full operating range (0.5–50 L/min) without sensor reconfiguration
needs.satisfactionRationale: Mag handles full range (100:1 turndown); PW limited but functional

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of false flow readings caused by air bubble entrainment during glycol system refill or maintenance events
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW jams/stalls with air; mag less affected but still errors

needs.statement: Minimize the time to detect a glycol jacket blockage or partial restriction before fermenter temperature deviates >0.5°C from setpoint
needs.satisfactionRationale: No incumbent provides glycol jacket flow monitoring; manual inspection only

needs.statement: Minimize the number of undetected glycol pump degradation events that reduce per-zone cooling capacity by >15%
needs.satisfactionRationale: No incumbent trends pump degradation; manual checks only

needs.statement: Increase the ability to distinguish genuine process flow deviations from sensor measurement noise with >95% classification accuracy
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW has poor S/N (dominant); mag better but no classification logic

needs.statement: Minimize the time to detect a glycol leak in the distribution system before coolant volume loss exceeds 5% of reservoir capacity
needs.satisfactionRationale: No automated leak detection in incumbent brewery glycol setups

needs.statement: Increase the ability to determine the correct glycol flow rate setpoint within ±10% of actual cooling load for each fermenter size and beer style
needs.satisfactionRationale: Knowledge/engineering need; sensor-independent

needs.statement: Increase the ability to calculate required glycol flow rates for different beer styles and fermenter sizes from a single sensor configuration
needs.satisfactionRationale: Knowledge need; sensor-independent

needs.statement: Minimize the time required to install and commission a flow sensor on an existing glycol line without draining the system
needs.satisfactionRationale: All inline sensors require system drain for installation

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of systematic measurement error >1% caused by incorrect fluid-type configuration at commissioning
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW needs no fluid config (zero error risk); mag minimal config

needs.statement: Increase the ability to verify sensor accuracy within 5 minutes before starting a fermentation batch
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW has no self-diagnostics; mag basic only; mostly skipped in practice

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading that masks a partially blocked glycol jacket
needs.satisfactionRationale: No incumbent detects partial jacket blockage — completely unaddressed

needs.statement: Increase the ability to isolate flow anomaly root cause (sensor vs glycol pump vs zone valve) within 5 minutes
needs.satisfactionRationale: No incumbent provides diagnostic data for root cause isolation

needs.statement: Minimize the operator expertise required to interpret flow anomaly data and determine the correct corrective action
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW: no diagnostics; mag: complex outputs requiring training

needs.statement: Increase the sensor service life achievable without scheduled maintenance interventions affecting measurement accuracy (target: >3 years)
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW bearing life 6–18 mo in glycol dominates; mag >10yr but minority

needs.statement: Minimize the total installed cost per glycol flow measurement point including sensor, wiring, and power supply (target: <$500)
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW at $50–150 well under target; mag at $800–2,000 exceeds it

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of sensor-induced flow path contamination requiring line flushing after sensor installation or replacement
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW: some debris risk; mag: clean. Mixed

needs.statement: Minimize the permanent pressure drop across the measurement point that increases glycol pump energy consumption
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW/mag: minimal drop; orifice: high. Mixed

needs.statement: Minimize batch-to-batch variation in fermentation temperature profile caused by uneven glycol distribution across cooling zones (target: <±0.5°C)
needs.satisfactionRationale: Without per-zone flow monitoring, glycol distribution is uncontrolled

needs.statement: Increase the ability to cool multiple fermenters simultaneously without flow starvation to any individual cooling zone
needs.satisfactionRationale: No per-zone flow visibility → starvation undetected until temp drifts

needs.statement: Increase the ability to present quantitative cooling system performance data to brewery ownership and quality assurance teams
needs.satisfactionRationale: PW: basic pulse only; mag: better data but too expensive to deploy at every point

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible
needs.satisfactionRationale: No flow-based early warning exists; relies on slow-response fermenter temp probe

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of false flow readings when air entrainment exceeds 2% volume during glycol system refill or valve-switching events
Minimize the time to recover valid flow measurement after transient air entrainment events dissipate from the glycol circuit
Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation from insufficient straight pipe run in space-constrained brewery glycol manifolds with elbows and T-junctions within 5–10 DN upstream
Minimize the duration of measurement blackout caused by partial pipe drainage during glycol circuit maintenance
Minimize the transient measurement error during crash-cooling sequences where glycol temperature changes exceed the compensation algorithm response rate at >2°C/min
Minimize the likelihood of signal-to-noise degradation below the transit-time detection threshold during low-demand glycol flow in small cooling zones
Minimize the risk of acoustic coupling failure when glycol-to-water ratio shifts outside the validated acoustic impedance range due to uncontrolled top-up or evaporation

sources.label: JTBD_breweries.md (24 error statements, incumbent anchors, alternatives, ODI pre-scores)
FIT_breweries.md (7 product-related needs from Step 10, 7 tech feature-market fit scores from Step 09)
